LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS MICHAEL SCHINNER, AND SCHINNER & SHAIN, LLP'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. OFF CALENDAR. ON FEBRUARY 15, 2024, THIS COURT ISSUED AN ORDER STAYING THIS ACTION. THE ORDER PROVIDES THAT "[T]HIS CASE IS THEREFORE STAYED PENDING FURTHER ORDER OF THE COURT." (ORDER RE DEFENDANTS SCHINNER AND SCHINNER & SHAIN LLP'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, 2:9-10.) THERE IS NOTHING IN THE RECORD SHOWING THAT THIS COURT ISSUED A SUBSEQUENT ORDER LIFTING THE STAY. THE IMPROPERLY NOTICED MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS THEREFORE OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (301/JMQ)

ORDER RE DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-15-2024 HEARING RE DEFENDANT MICHAEL SCHINNER, SCHINNER & SHAIN, LLP'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PURSUANT TO CCP 128(A)(8), THE COURT SUA SPONTE STAYS THIS CASE PENDING DISPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF'S PETITION IN THE CASE OF IN RE THE AMENDED AND RESTATED KRIKORIAN FAMILY TRUST DATED AUGUST 29, 2002, SAN MATEO COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT CASE NO. 22-PR-01155 (THE "PROBATE CASE"). ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SIGNED ORDER/MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MICHAEL SCHINNER, AND SCHINNER & SHAIN, LLP'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Off calendar. On February 15, 2024, this court issued an order staying this action. The order provides that "[t]his case is therefore stayed pending further order of the court." (Order re Defendants Schinner and Schinner & Shain LLP's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings, 2:9-10.) There is nothing in the record showing that this court issued a subsequent order lifting the stay. The improperly noticed motion for judgment on the pleadings is therefore off calendar.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Continued To Aug-19-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-15-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. PAUL FRASSETTO, Esq. of Frassetto Law Offices (Tel: 415-307-5383) for Plaintiff.
Atty. KRISTIN IVERSEN, Esq. of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415-788-1900) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MICHAEL SCHINNER, SCHINNER & SHAIN, LLP's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The Court passes the matter. The Court recalls the matter. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Pursuant to CCP 128(a)(8), the court sua sponte stays this case pending disposition of plaintiff's petition in the case of In re the Amended and Restated Krikorian Family Trust dated August 29, 2002, San Mateo County Superior Court Case No. 22-PR-01155 (the "Probate Case").

In this attorney malpractice case, plaintiff alleges that defendant helped amend the family trust in such a manner that plaintiff's mother's testamentary intent was undermined. (Complaint, pars. 15, 17.) Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings argues that defendant did not act negligently and the Third Amendment accurately expressed plaintiff's mother's intent. (Defendant's P&A, 13:12-28.) Plaintiff's allegations are contrary to this assertion.

In Paul v. Patton (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1088, 1098, the court held that the testator's attorney owed a duty to the beneficiaries when the attorney's drafting error in an executed trust amendment resulted in the failure to carry out the testator's wishes.

Based on this authority and for the sake of judicial economy, the court concludes that the Probate Case should be resolved before this action proceeds any further. It is clear that resolution of the Probate Case will affect this action. This case is therefore stayed pending further order of the court.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
